You need JavaScript enabled to view it. : Detailed Profile About Faculty: Dr. Krishnakant Dhakar has done his B.E. and M.E. from Jabalpur Engineering College, Jabalpur and Punjab Engineering College, Chandigarh respectively. He obtained his Ph.D. from Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Roorkee. He has served as an Assistant Professor at NIT Bhopal and NIT Jalandhar. His research interests include Advanced Machining, Composite Materials, Optimization and Supply Chain Management. He has authored or co-authored more than 35 publications which include journal papers, conference papers, and book chapters. He has also got two patents. He has been PI/Co-PI for five projects and two consultancies. He has been the reviewer of many reputed journals. Further, he has been the guest editor of Materials Today’s. He has presented his research at USA and Japan. Education and Qualification: S. No. Degree Specialization Year University/Board 1. Ph.D. Advanced Machining 2016 IIT Roorkee 2. M.E. Production Engineering 2009 PEC Chandigarh 3. B.E. Industrial & Production Engg. 2007 JEC Jabalpur Work Experience: S. No. Post Institute Year 1. Assistant Professor SGSITS, INDORE Oct 25 Till now 2. Assistant Professor MANIT, Bhopal July 2016 to Oct. 2018 3. Assistant Professor NIT Jalandhar Jan 2010 to June 2012 Research Details: Research Interests Advanced Machining, Composite Materials, Non-traditional machining processes, Optimization Publications: 1. Research Paper Publications (I) International/National Journal Publications 1. Rajak U., Chaurasiya P., Verma T., Dhakar K. , Nagdeve L. (2021) “Numerical Study on the Effect of Engine Characteristics by Using Ethanol-Moringa-Diesel Fuel” Kuwait Journal of Science (I.F. 0.65) 2. Nagdeve L., Dhakar K., Kumar H. (2020) “Development of novel finishing tool into Magnetic Abrasive Finishing process of Aluminum 6061” Materials and Manufacturing Processes, Vol. 35 (10); pp. 1129-1134 (I.F. 3.35). 3. P Chaurasiya, P Pandey, U Rajak, K Dhakar , M Verma, T Verma “Epidemic and challenges of coronavirus disease-2019 (COVID-19): India response” https://dx.doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.3569665 4. Dhakar K., Kumar A., Mishra P. ( SCOPUS) 5. Dhakar K., Chaudhary K., Dvivedi A. and Bembelge O.
